exact_milestones.result: published Paper-1 claims are separated from the kneading admissibility hypothesis
exact parity-renormalized cycle counts and noncommuting long-cycle/small-noise limits
exact two-channel critical-branch factorization and dark-mode algebra
exact sparse two-step Grushin linearization with stored inverse certificates
gauge-invariant weighted Riesz projector continuum bridge
directional Hardy/Stein theorem and global-contraction no-go
phase-aware finite-horizon plus positive Stein-tail completion
Schur-secular correction certificate without ambient reference packet
source-seed equivalence and full-prefix recursive horizon theorem
projector envelope and strict positive unit-propagation counterexample

executive_verdict: The hundred-layer program has produced a substantial exact finite-dimensional theory, a fully validated five-anchor Stage-A chain, and a precise moving-cloud determinant frontier. The preferred packet corridor is still viable, but RH-95--RH-99 show that smooth uniform propagation is the wrong default: weak modes, cumulative quotient losses, nonunit propagation, and unavailable Ritz gaps must be handled explicitly. The next best route is the stopped-hybrid packet bundle, while the RH-75 all-level full-block law remains the clean fallback.
